clustered_themes: Escalation timing is a decisive variable in termination risk control.
Route/evidence/payroll misalignment should trigger immediate escalation, not routine queueing.
Manager script drift and local-interpretation gaps are early-warning signals.
Closure evidence quality remains critical for post-event defensibility.
source_fit_review.deduplication_note: Rotated from play-call sequence to escalation-map format focused on trigger thresholds and owner escalation routes.
key_uncertainties: Settlement tax outcomes vary by component design and local handling.
Late factual discoveries can force route changes.
